fp_examples: Mais quel est l'objectif ultime de cette dérogation ? S'agit-il de sortir, quoi qu'il en coûte, de l'indivision, quitte à accentuer les phénomènes de prédation foncière, une surenchère sans précédent, excluant un peuple de l'accès à la propriété de sa terre ? Tout ça va mal finir.
Nous vivons désormais dans un village global avec de nombreuses cases administratives en miettes vides, préoccupées à durer, quoi qu'il en coûte.
true_birth_in_corpus: 2020-03-19 — Éric Woerth (LR), Commission des finances, examen du PLFR 2020, explicitly quoting Macron: "pour reprendre les mots du Président de la République, quoi qu'il en coûte" (7 days after the 2020-03-12 allocution; 4 more genuine uses the same day: Roussel x2, Rabault, Saint-Martin)
birth_notes: Expected birth confirmed: no genuine parliamentary use predates Macron's 2020-03-12 allocution. Pre-birth idiomatic background is nearly nil — only 2 plain-idiom hits in ~2.5 years (Potier 2018-07-18; Jacob 2018-07-31, fused spelling "quoiqu'il en coûte" which the 'quoi ?qu' pattern correctly catches), giving a very clean baseline-vs-explosion signal. The formula enters the AN via OPPOSITION quotation, not the majority: first echoes are Woerth (LR), Roussel (PCF), Rabault (PS) quoting Macron back on 2020-03-19 during PLFR1 — the left immediately weaponizes it as a commitment device ("vérifier que l'engagement... sera vraiment mis en œuvre"). Nominalization with article ("le « quoi qu'il en coûte » du Président") first appears 2020-06-02 (Pirès Beaune), marking full lexicalization as a political object ~3 months after birth.
notes: FP rate ~4% (2/45), all late-corpus (2026) plain-adverbial idiom with no fiscal resonance — inherent bleed for a phrase that IS ordinary French; do not tighten, since requiring the nominalized "le/du quoi qu'il en coute" would kill genuine adverbial echoes (Woerth's founding quotation, Rabault, Vidal, Battistel, Nadeau). Clear lifecycle arc worth featuring in the analysis: (1) 2020 opposition quotes it back as a promissory note; (2) 2021 government owns and nominalizes it (Borne, Le Maire "la politique du quoi qu'il en coûte que nous avions décidée") then builds the exit narrative ("ciblé et dégressif", "sortis du quoi qu'il en coûte"); (3) 2023-2024 retrospective blame frame from LR (Di Filippo "fardeau financier", Juvin's commission d'enquête on debt); (4) 2024 productive template extension by Écologistes ("un quoi qu'il en coûte écologique/éducatif/scolaire"); (5) 2025-2026 fade-out into ordinary idiom plus bitter nominal retrospectives ("payer la facture du quoi qu'il en coûte"). Venue skews heavily Commission des finances. RN nearly absent from samples (one meta use, Guiniot 2024-11-26 "pour reprendre la formule consacrée"). Data-quality flags for the corpus: the 2023-04-26 quote attributed to Bruno Le Maire contains "comme Bruno Le Maire l'a dit" — speaker attribution likely wrong (probably the rapporteur); party_name typo "Les Récuplicains" appears twice.

fp_examples: Nous sommes en guerre économique.

Cette convention vise-t-elle à aider le port de Dunkerque et les ports français en général ou à créer une autoroute fluviale vers les ports d’Anvers et de Rotterdam, contre lesquels nous sommes en guerre permanente ?

Donc pour revenir sur la question aussi du député, monsieur le député, c'est sûr que nous sommes en guerre, qu'on le veuille ou non, nous sommes indirectement en guerre parce que les Iraniens nous visent.

pattern_fixes.detail: guerre economique — kills the fixed collocation 'nous sommes en guerre economique' (2017-12-07 journalists, Loubet RN 2025-03-27, CE reindustrialisation 2025-03-27); no genuine COVID quote uses it
guerre permanente — kills the Hutin 2021-01-13 port-rivalry metaphor ('en guerre permanente' contre Anvers/Rotterdam); never appears in genuine COVID usage

economie de guerre — kills the post-2022 Ukraine/rearmament register (Lecornu Eurosatory 2022-07-07, Bourouaha 2026-02-25) which dodges the existing 'ukraine'/'russie' excludes because those words are absent from the quotes
\blibye\b — literal war against Libya (2022-12-15 energy CE)
\biran(ien(ne)?s?)?\b — literal Iran threat ('les iraniens nous visent', 2026-04-01 affaires etrangeres)
si nous etions en guerre — hypothetical literal-war conditional in defense debates (Jacques 2023-05-09); genuine quotations of Macron all use 'que nous etions en guerre' and are unaffected
signifierait que nous sommes en guerre — conditional literal-war reasoning in the conscription debate (Saintoul 2026-05-18)

true_birth_in_corpus: 2020-03-19 — Daniel Labaronne (LREM/Renaissance), commission des finances, PLFR 2020: "la guerre sanitaire contre le coronavirus" — first genuine talking-point use, 3 days after Macron's 2020-03-16 allocution (matched via the 'guerre sanitaire' pattern; first genuine 'nous sommes en guerre' string is Serville quoting Macron on 2020-06-18).
birth_notes: Expected birth confirmed: no genuine pre-Macron COVID use in the corpus; the parliamentary echo starts 2020-03-19 (Labaronne, majority, sincere adoption of 'guerre sanitaire'). But the corpus also documents the rhetorical baseline the formula rose from: Gauvain (LREM) 2017-10-11 'Mes chers collegues, nous sommes en guerre' in the counter-terrorism (SILT) debate — the post-2015-attacks war register — and Boyer (LR) 2018-04-06 in the immigration/asylum debate. These pre-birth uses are genuine linguistic background, not FPs; note the 'terrorisme' exclude misses Gauvain because the word appears only in the session title, not the quote text. There is also a persistent pre- and post-birth 'guerre economique' background stream (2017-12-07 onward) that is a distinct idiom and should be excluded.
notes: Counts are over the 33 unique quotes (45 rows include 12 duplicates between first-15 and random samples, plus one doubled row: the 2024-10-24 affaires-economiques quote appears twice verbatim — possible corpus dedup issue). FP rate ~27% pre-fix; the 7 proposed excludes remove all 9 FPs without touching any genuine quote. Striking usage pattern: after the first weeks (Labaronne sincere), the formula survives in the AN almost exclusively as QUOTATION or IRONY by the opposition turning Macron's metaphor against the government — LFI (Rubin 'puisqu'il parait que nous sommes en guerre', Ruffin, Panot 'repete six fois', Guette), LR (Dalloz 'formule tres malheureuse'), de Courson ('arretons les grands mots'), PS (Califer) — typically to demand war-style obligations (war-profits taxes, state support). Hotspot: the 2021-04-14 finances commission on the 'taxe sur les profiteurs de crise' yields 4 matches in one sitting, all meta-debate about the formula. Venue skew: heavily commission-based rather than seance publique. Second-life contamination: from 2022 the same string re-anchors to the Ukraine/rearmament register ('economie de guerre') and from 2025 to literal war threats (Iran, conscription) — the existing 'ukraine'/'russie' excludes are insufficient because speakers rarely name those words; consider prophylactic excludes ('otan', 'poutine', 'kiev') if more post-2022 contamination shows up, though only evidence-backed fixes are proposed here. Remaining ambiguous stream (6 quotes) is mostly this post-2022 drift plus context-free one-liners; acceptable residue for the covid-etat-protecteur subject.

fp_examples: Après les symptômes arrive le diagnostic puis, s’abattant telle une enclume, la voix du médecin annonce la fatalité : les jours heureux de notre enfant sont comptés, sa vie normale est compromise, celle que ces copains d’école, eux, auront la joie de vivre.
Attaquées souvent par des groupuscules d'extrême droite, je voulais vous donner un exemple dans ma circonscription : la librairie Les Jours Heureux à Ronnie Soubois en juin dernier.

Vous avez écouté quelles que soient les conditions, leurs origines, et au fil des ans, vous l'avez dit, vos journaux sont devenus une sorte de radioscopie de la France, de ces jours heureux et de ces moments de peine.

pattern_fixes.detail: librairie — excludes mentions of the bookshop 'Les Jours Heureux' (proper name of a business, seen 2026-01-21); safe, no genuine quote contains it

coul\w+ des jours heureux — preventive: the very common literal idiom 'couler des jours heureux' (to live out happy days) is near-certain in the wider corpus and never the CNR/Macron formula; not observed in this sample but cheap and safe

true_birth_in_corpus: 2019-12-17 — Mathilde Panot (LFI), "pacte des jours heureux" in commission des affaires européennes, immediately debated by minister Amélie de Montchalin the same session; explicit CNR anchoring follows with Sébastien Jumel (GDR/PCF) 2020-02-03 and Fabien Roussel 2020-02-18 — all months BEFORE Macron's expected birth of 2020-04-13
birth_notes: The corpus inverts the expected birth story. Macron's 2020-04-13 allocution is the famous public birth, but in the Assemblée the formula was already a live left-wing revival: Panot (LFI) coins a "pacte des jours heureux" on 2019-12-17 (and Montchalin engages with it), then the communists explicitly reclaim the CNR program during the pension-reform battle (Jumel 2020-02-03: "Ambroise Croizat et les résistants ont écrit Les Jours heureux"; Roussel 2020-02-18: "nous avons écrit le programme des jours heureux"). So Macron adopted a formula the parliamentary left had re-armed months earlier — not the reverse. Pre-birth ordinary-idiom background exists but is thin: one ironic Goasguen quote (2018-04-04, "Vous vous préparez des jours heureux"), which is baseline idiom, not the formula. Macron himself never appears (presidents do not speak in the AN); his adoption surfaces indirectly via majority speakers echoing the covid-reopening frame (Gouffier Valente 2021-05-25 "retour aux jours heureux, à la vie normale").
notes: 29 unique quotes judged (random_samples duplicates the entire first-15). FP rate ~10%, all late (2023+), all semantically idiosyncratic literal uses (sick child, bookshop name, happy-days-vs-sorrows); only the bookshop is regex-excludable, residual ~7% idiom noise is acceptable for a common collocation. Ambiguous: Attal 2024-01-30 uses "promettaient des jours heureux" about Brexit — generic idiom, possibly a deliberate sideways echo. Usage sociology is sharp: sincere use is a left monopoly (LFI + PCF/GDR: Fiat x3, Obono, Ratenon, Larive, Guetté, Boyard, Sansu, Lebon's colleagues), tied to Sécurité sociale anniversaries and the Obono resolution "bifurcation écologique et solidaire pour aller vers les jours heureux" (June 2020), which put the phrase into procedural language (Renson, Genevard reading the order of the day). Majority/government uses are ironic or quotational (Montchalin, Poirson "vos jours heureux... lendemains qui déchantent", Naegelen on public finances), with exactly one sincere majority use in Macron's reopening frame (Gouffier Valente 2021-05-25). Venue pattern: born in commissions (Lois, Affaires européennes, retraite spéciale), then migrates to séance publique from Feb 2020. The phrase remains active through 2026 (Boyard, Sansu 2026-06-09), always as the left's horizon formula.

fp_examples: Oui, si nous regardons ses ressources, l'augmentation du budget de la défense, la double augmentation du budget de la diplomatie chinoise entre 2011 et 2018 – la Chine vient de devenir le premier réseau diplomatique au monde d'après le nombre de représentations à l'étranger –, au regard aussi du dynamisme de cette diplomatie qui est professionnelle et qui peut donc s'appuyer non seulement sur des ressources financières importantes, mais aussi sur des ressources humaines importantes et bien formées.
true_birth_in_corpus: 2020-04-15 — Amélie de Montchalin (Gouvernement, secrétaire d'État aux affaires européennes), Commission des affaires étrangères: "Les femmes et les représentants des différentes catégories sociales doivent avoir toute leur place dans les instances de réflexion sur le monde d'après." Same day, an unidentified speaker in Commission des lois already uses it meta ("On parle souvent du monde d'après"), confirming the formula arrived in the AN as an established commonplace.
birth_notes: Corpus birth is consistent with the expected diffuse mid-March 2020 birth, with a ~1-month parliamentary lag (Assemblée activity was suspended during early confinement). The 2019-12-11 raw first match is a pure syntactic false positive ("premier réseau diplomatique au monde, d'après le nombre de représentations" — d'après = according to), NOT an early use. One pre-birth idiomatic background hit exists: 2020-02-05, a hearing witness on judicial independence says "un monde d'avant et un monde d'après la loi du 25 juillet 2013" — the avant/après-monde template predates COVID as ordinary French, which is the baseline the political formula rises from. First genuine use is government-side (Montchalin, 15 Apr 2020), but the formula is immediately captured by the left opposition: on 16 Apr 2020 in the PLFR2 Finances debate, LFI's Sabine Rubin uses it 4 times in one sitting, joined by Roussel (GDR) and Coquerel (LFI), turning it into a demand the executive live up to its own promise; the rapporteur général Saint-Martin answers inside the frame the same day.
notes: Striking patterns: (1) The formula becomes ironic/adversarial within weeks — by May-June 2020 it appears mostly in scare quotes or with distancing markers: "le fameux monde d'après" (Fiat, LFI), "un concept à la mode" (Beaudouin-Hubiere, LREM, mocking the CGT), "Vous nous vendiez un « monde d'après » merveilleux" (Coquerel), "Plutôt que du « monde d'après », je préfère parler des leçons..." (witness). Sincere prescriptive use is concentrated on the left (LFI/GDR/PS: green relance, ISF, public services, partage de la valeur), while the majority uses it defensively or quotes Macron. (2) Le Drian's pessimistic counter-version ("le monde d'après sera plus dur") circulates as its own sub-formula (Habib 22 Apr, Le Drian himself 20 May). (3) Long afterlife: still used sincerely in 2022 (Potier), given a retrospective obituary by Batho in 2023 ("loin de... la construction d'un monde d'après... effet rebond"), self-citation by Waserman (his 2020 book "Le monde d'après commence demain matin"), and reused for a maritime-transport crisis in a 2026 hearing (Lhardit, PS) — formula migration to a new crisis. Caveat on that 2026 quote: it says "trois mois après le début de la crise", and its raw-transcript typos suggest auto-transcription; worth verifying date/attribution, though Lhardit (elected 2024) fits a 2026 shipping-crisis hearing. The single FP type ("au monde, d'après [source]" = according-to) is ~2% and cannot be excluded without killing genuine "réfléchir/penser au monde d'après" constructions — keep patterns as-is. Optional recall extension (not required): a separate pattern "jour d'apres" would capture direct echoes of Macron's 16 March address ("le jour d'après ne sera pas un retour au jour d'avant"), but it carries its own noise and is better treated as a sibling phrase than folded into this one. Metadata nit seen twice in samples: party_name misspelled "Les Récuplicains" (Thiériot 2020-07-09, Perrut 2021-03-24).

true_birth_in_corpus: 2020-02-10 — Jacques Maire (LREM), commission spéciale retraites: "Nous assumons l'instauration de l'âge d'équilibre parce qu'il n'y a pas d'argent magique." Immediately retourné the same day, same sitting, by Adrien Quatennens (LFI).
birth_notes: Expected birth (Macron, 2018-04-05, CHU Rouen) is extra-parliamentary and predates all corpus matches. Despite the corpus starting in 2017, there is ZERO AN usage between April 2018 and February 2020 — no ordinary-idiom background exists; "argent magique" was not a pre-existing parliamentary idiom. It enters the AN via the pension-reform commission (2020-02-10) as a same-sitting duel: sincere pro-government use by Jacques Maire (LREM) instantly turned back by Quatennens (LFI). It then explodes mid-2020 with Covid, systematically paired with "quoi qu'il en coûte" (Juanico, Di Filippo, Roussel, Panot) — the retournement "pas d'argent magique pour l'hôpital / quoi qu'il en coûte pour le CAC40" becomes the dominant frame. Bonus: Leduc (2022-12-13) misdates the Macron scene to "pendant la pandémie" (it was 2018), showing the formula's memory drifting into the Covid frame.
notes: Pattern is exceptionally clean: "argent magique" has no competing literal sense — all 39 unique quotes (45 read, 6 duplicates between first-15 and random samples) are the political formula, its retournement, or meta-debate about it; even garbled ASR fragments ("N'y pas d'argent magique.", 2026-04-29) and commission-hearing witnesses (2025-05-28, 2026-05-28) are genuine downstream uses. Empty exclude list is correct; no fixes needed. Venue/actor patterns: LFI is the heaviest user (Quatennens, Fiat, Obono, Panot x2, Leduc, Bernalicis, Clouet), nearly always ironic; right/majority invert it to accuse the left of magic-money thinking (Di Filippo 2020, Minot and Maillard 2024, RN's Renault 2026); by 2025-26 it is fully lexicalized as a generic budget idiom used by unattributed commission speakers and re-adopted sincerely by the government (Bregeon, QAG 2026-03-24). Early life is commission-heavy (retraites, dette sociale, finances), moving to séance publique from mid-2020.

fp_examples: Un président de la République dont on célébrait récemment le cinquantième anniversaire du décès avait dit : « Arrêtez donc d'emmerder les Français ! » Moi, je vous dis : arrêtez d'emmerder les paysans !

Alors, je veux dire, au bout d'un moment, arrêtez cette idéologie contre tout, contre le plastique, etc. Ça commence vraiment à emmerder les Français. Donc, à un moment donné, il faut arrêter de s'opposer à tout, et il faut aussi être capable de soutenir notre industrie.

Comme disait le président Pompidou, il faut arrêter d'emmerder les Français, notamment les agriculteurs : pour eux, les transpositions sont sans pitié ; pour assurer la sécurité des Français et expulser les étrangers en situation irrégulière, c'est une autre histoire !

pattern_fixes.detail: (arret|cess)[a-z]*( donc| enfin)* d'emmerder — removes Pompidou's 1966 imperative formula ('arrêtez/cesser d'emmerder les Français'), a distinct parent talking point running continuously 2018-2026 (norms, farmers, plastic); all 8 genuine Macron-formula matches survive since none use the imperative construction.

pompidou[^.]{0,100}emmerder les francais — catches explicit Pompidou attributions phrased outside the imperative (e.g. Perrier 2025-05-27: 'où Pompidou disait… arrêtait d'emmerder les Français'); proximity-bound so a hypothetical genuine quote contrasting Pompidou with 'emmerder les non-vaccinés' is not lost.

a emmerder les francais — kills generic-idiom noise ('ça commence vraiment à emmerder les Français', 2025-12-10 plastics debate); no genuine match uses the 'à emmerder' construction (genuine forms are d'emmerder / vouloir emmerder / envie de les emmerder). Small residual risk of losing a future 'cherche à emmerder les Français' aimed at Macron — lowest-priority of the three.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2022-01-04 — Sébastien Jumel (GDR/Communistes), 2ème séance publique on the passe vaccinal ('il s'agit d'emmerder les Français, de cliver le pays et de fracturer la société'), the very day/night Macron's Le Parisien interview was published mid-debate. Matches the expected birth to the day; first parliamentary echo within hours.
birth_notes: The corpus reveals a two-generation formula family. Pre-birth (2018-06-13 → 2021-11-25), all 9 matches are Pompidou's 1966 'Arrêtez d'emmerder les Français !', cited cross-party (PS Saulignac, LR Gosselin/Sermier, MoDem Millienne, LREM Touraine, UDI Bricout/Petit, RN Meizonnet via Pompili) as anti-norm/anti-bureaucracy rhetoric — this is genuine background, the baseline Macron deliberately inverted. Notably the Pompidou frame was already turned against COVID policy on 2020-10-29 ('Ce Gouvernement va-t-il cesser enfin d'emmerder les Français ?', read out by Barrot in the sanitary-situation debate) — 14 months before Macron flipped it. The true birth matches the expected story exactly: Jumel reacts on 2022-01-04, the day the interview dropped, followed by a dense cluster (Panot and Abadie 2022-01-05 in commission des lois, Meizonnet 2022-01-11, Ruffin 2022-01-16 quoting the exact 'j'ai très envie de les emmerder', Untermaier 2022-01-26 already transferring it to a non-COVID domain 'expression désormais célèbre'). Key mechanism: parliament instantly re-translated 'les non-vaccinés… les emmerder' back into the Pompidou surface form 'emmerder les Français' (Jumel, Meizonnet, Abadie) — which is why the 'francais' branch is needed for genuine coverage, and why the pattern also drags in the parent formula. Post-2023, the Macron formula appears only as memory (S. Rousseau 2023-04, Diaz 2023-10 listing it among Macron's 'petites phrases'), while the Pompidou parent formula resumes its independent life (2024-2026: paysans, réindustrialisation, plastique) — currently misattributed to this phrase. Recommend a separate precursor phrase entry (e.g. arretez-d-emmerder-les-francais, pattern '(arret|cess)[a-z]*( donc| enfin)* d.emmerder les francais') so the baseline the excludes remove stays in the genealogy.
notes: 24 unique quotes audited (first-15 and random samples overlap on 15). Genuine = 8 post-birth talking-point uses + 9 pre-birth Pompidou baseline (counted genuine background per audit rules). False positives = 5, all post-birth revivals of the Pompidou parent formula in non-COVID contexts (Le Fur 2024-05-23 paysans, Perrier 2025-05-27 réindustrialisation, Taverne 2025-06-25 agriculteurs, anonymous 2025-12-10 plastique, Dragon 2026-07-02) — 21% FP rate, and worse, they fake a 2024-2026 afterlife for a phrase that actually died in 2023. Ambiguous = 2: Aurélien Rousseau 2024-11-04 (anti-tobacco measures 'là pour emmerder les Français' — health-paternalism frame adjacent to the covid-etat-protecteur subject but not the Macron formula) and Fabrice Brun 2025-01-28 ('Et surtout d'emmerder les Français !' — context-free interjection, unfixable by regex). Usage pattern: the Macron formula is exclusively hostile quotation — LFI, GDR, RN, PS attack with it; Renaissance (Abadie) engages only defensively; zero sincere adoption. RN's Meizonnet sits on both sides of the inversion (used the Pompidou frame pre-birth in March 2021, the anti-Macron version in Jan 2022). Venue: birth and spike in séance publique during the passe-vaccinal marathon, echoes in commission des lois. The 'non-vaccines' branch and 'envie de les emmerder' pattern are 3/3 clean and should be kept untouched. Data quirk worth fixing upstream: party_name 'Les Récuplicains' (typo for Républicains) appears repeatedly in corpus metadata.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2017-07-05 — Adrien Quatennens (LFI), Commission des affaires sociales
birth_notes: The expected birth (Mélenchon/Parti de Gauche, 2008) predates the corpus, so the first corpus match cannot show it; but the corpus perfectly confirms the expected trajectory. First occurrence is 2017-07-05 by Adrien Quatennens (LFI), weeks into the legislature — and ALL of the first 15 matches (2017-07 to 2018-10) are LFI speakers (Quatennens, Obono, Prud'homme, Panot, Bernalicis), mostly in commissions, often tied to LFI's constitutional agenda ('règle verte', a proposed constitutional title 'De la planification écologique'). The phrase has no ordinary-idiom background: it enters the corpus already as the LFI formula. Macron's 2022-04-16 capture is then visible in the samples: the first non-LFI use in evidence is Christophe Béchu (Gouvernement) on 2022-09-13, followed by full institutionalization — SGPE ('secrétariat général à la planification écologique'), PLF budget action 29 named 'Planification écologique', a 'conseil de planification écologique', and the PM's official title 'chargé de la planification écologique et énergétique' read out by the chair from 2025.
notes: Patterns are clean as-is; the secondary pattern 'planifier la transition ecologique' fired once (Panot, 2018-10-17) and is genuine — keep it. Striking analytic story: a textbook two-phase genealogy. Phase 1 (2017-2021): exclusively LFI, oppositional, programmatic, commission-heavy. Phase 2 (post-April-2022 capture): cross-spectrum — Gouvernement (Béchu, Guerini, Lescure, Fesneau, Pannier-Runacher), Renaissance (Brulebois, Girardin, Cazeneuve, Fugit), MoDem, Écologistes — and the formula becomes state nomenclature (SGPE, budget line, ministerial portfolio). LFI keeps using it post-capture but increasingly as a reproach that the government betrays its own borrowed word (Chikirou 2022 'double discours', Meunier 2024 'quel avenir réservez-vous à la planification écologique'). One measurement caveat, not a pattern flaw: 3 of 30 samples are presiding-officer boilerplate ('La parole est à M. le premier ministre, chargé de la planification écologique et énergétique'), so raw post-2024 frequency is mildly inflated by procedural title mentions; if the analysis wants purely discursive adoption, it could tag (not exclude) the normalized fragment 'charge de la planification ecologique et energetique' as institutional-title mentions — they are still genuine evidence of institutionalization, the endpoint of the capture.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2018-03-07, Loïc Prud'homme (LFI), commission du développement durable (audition d'Arnaud Leroy pour la présidence de l'ADEME) — first genuine use in corpus, as pre-2022 ecological-policy background. First occurrence of the 2022 governmental doctrine variant ('plan de sobriété') visible in the samples: 2022-09-13, Agnès Pannier-Runacher (commission des affaires économiques).
birth_notes: The expected birth (2022-10-06 plan Borne) is NOT the birth of the phrase in the corpus — it is a frequency spike plus a doctrinal rebranding. 'Sobriété énergétique' circulates continuously from 2018-03-07 (Prud'homme, LFI), used sincerely by LFI/écologistes/GDR/PS (Corbière even tried to write it into the 2018 loi de programmation militaire), and is already contested as a formula on 2018-03-13 by Jean-Jacques Bridey (LREM), who says he prefers 'performance énergétique' — early majority resistance to the word. Édouard Philippe uses it in his déclaration de politique générale on 2019-06-12, i.e. governmental adoption 3+ years before the Borne plan. What 2022 actually births is the 'plan de sobriété' variant as state doctrine (first sampled: Pannier-Runacher 2022-09-13, referencing the plan announced 'avant l'été' — even slightly before the official 2022-10-06 presentation). The corpus even contains an explicit genealogy: Pannier-Runacher (2022-10-05) credits négaWatt and Réseau action climat as co-authors of the plan de sobriété — the militant-ecology origin acknowledged from the government bench. So the story is: militant/left coinage → left parliamentary background (2018-19) → governmental capture (2019 Philippe, consecrated 2022 Borne/Pannier-Runacher) → all-party mainstreaming and domain diffusion (eau: Béchu, Guerini 2023).
notes: Patterns are clean: 44/45 quotes genuine, 1 ambiguous (~2%), 0 false positives — no fixes needed. The one ambiguous hit (2025-10-22, unidentified, Finances: 'plans de sobriété et de réutilisation des sceaux [sic]') is 'plan de sobriété' applied to water, itself a spin-off of the energy formula, so arguably still part of the genealogy. Striking patterns: (1) venue is overwhelmingly commissions, séance publique appearances are rare and late (Philippe 2019 DPG, then 2025); (2) pre-2022 users skew LFI/écolo/GDR/PS with LR engaging critically (Aubert 2019 in the commission d'enquête on renewables); post-2022 the term is used by every family including Horizons and the government; (3) the 'sobriété choisie / subie' counter-formula pattern earns its keep — Lecoq (PCF, 2025-04-10) 'une sobriété choisie et non subie' is exactly the left's reclaiming move against the governmental framing, and Castor (2022-10-05) makes the overseas version ('la sobriété énergétique, nous la connaissons et la vivons depuis des dizaines d'années' = sobriété subie); (4) meta/ironic uses are a running thread: Bridey 2018 rejecting the word, Pompili 2023 'la sobriété énergétique – déjà !' noting the notion predates. Duplicate note: the Obono 2019-02-14 quote appears in both first-15 and random samples; counted once in totals (45 rows, 44+1 unique-classified).

true_birth_in_corpus: 2019-04-09 — Édouard Philippe (Premier ministre), séance publique, déclaration du Gouvernement sur le grand débat national: "ils ne voulaient pas opposer la fin du mois et la fin du monde."
birth_notes: Expected birth is the gilets-jaunes street slogan of Nov 2018 ("Fin du monde, fin du mois, même combat"). In the AN corpus the formula first appears 2019-04-09, spoken by PM Édouard Philippe restituting the Grand Débat — i.e. the EXECUTIVE imported the protest slogan into parliament, in a defensive "ne pas opposer" framing, five months after the public birth. The majority/government (Philippe, Poirson x2, Meynier-Millefert, Abad) dominate 2019 usage; the left only starts quoting the slogan verbatim from 2021-2022 (union table ronde, Trouvé, Coquerel), and the RN appears last (Blairy 2022, Le Pen 2025) using it INVERTED — fin du mois pitted against fin du monde, ecology as elite "culpabilité". No pre-birth idiomatic background exists in the corpus: pairing the two idioms within 100 chars is intrinsically the political formula, so there is no ordinary-idiom baseline to flag.
notes: Corpus contains only 14 unique matches total (first-15 list has 14 entries; random_samples are byte-identical to it — the sample is exhaustive). Precision is 100%: the two-sided proximity patterns (fins? d[eu] mois ↔ fin du monde within .{0,100}) leave no literal-sense surface, so no exclusions or tightening needed; empty exclude list is correct. Usage grammar splits cleanly by camp: government/majority = "ne pas opposer / réconcilier" (defensive reconciliation), left = "même combat" quoting the slogan approvingly (Potier, Trouvé, Coquerel), RN = hostile inversion (Blairy: "la fin du monde prévue par les sapiteurs"; Le Pen: "votre culpabilité de la fin du monde"). Venue skews to commissions (9/14: développement durable, finances, affaires économiques) with activity clustered in 2019 (loi énergie-climat, anti-gaspillage) and 2022 (energy crisis), then sporadic. Metadata quirks worth knowing for the analysis: Damien Abad (2019-10-10) is tagged party_name=Renaissance though family=LR (he was LR then); Marine Le Pen (2025-04-28) carries an EP-era party label ("Europe des Nations et des Libertés") and her quote has a transcription gap ("au de l'écologie", likely "au nom de l'écologie"). Two quotes are speaker-unidentified (2021-02-26 union representative; 2026-05-12, rhetorically left-wing). Low total volume means per-year trend claims should be made cautiously.

45 quotes read (15 first + 30 random), 43 unique — the Faucillon 2019-03-07 and Lacroute 2019-10-07 quotes are duplicated across the two lists. 100% precision: "écologie punitive" has no literal or other-sense reading; every match is either sincere deployment of the counter-formula, meta-discussion of the expression (Hulot 2017, Pompili 2018, de Rugy 2018), or defensive disavowal by the left ("nous ne sommes pas pour une écologie punitive" — Faucillon PCF 2019, Arrighi EELV 2022, Sas 2024), which is itself evidence the frame constrains its opponents. Striking pattern: ownership migration. 2017-2019 the phrase is trans-partisan (MoDem, LREM, LR, PCF), concentrated in commissions around carbon-tax/gilets-jaunes debates; from 2022-2023 it is heavily colonized by the RN (Meurin, de Fournas, Salmon, Guiniot, Renault, Humbert, Tanguy, Casterman, Dutremble — 9 of 15 attributed post-2022 samples), mostly in séance publique. Peak institutionalization: 2023-04-04, an official AN debate carries the formula in its title ("Débat sur l'impact de l'écologie punitive sur l'inflation et le pouvoir d'achat"), read into the record by the presiding officer (Rabault). Also sarcastic weaponization ("madame la ministre de l'écologie punitive", 2025-06-03). Auxiliary patterns (non punitive / de la punition / sobriete punitive) produced no sampled hits but pose no false-positive risk.
true_birth_in_corpus: 2017-07-25 — Bruno Millienne (MoDem), commission du développement durable, audition of minister Nicolas Hulot ("Tout comme l'écologie punitive, le changement des pratiques agricoles à marche forcée ne peut être productif.")
birth_notes: The expected birth (attested 2008, popularized by Ségolène Royal in 2014) predates the 2017-2026 corpus entirely, so the corpus cannot show the birth — it opens with the formula already mature and shared. First corpus match (Millienne, MoDem, 2017-07-25) uses it as established shorthand needing no explanation, and the second match is minister Nicolas Hulot himself (2017-10-03) meta-observing that "l'expression « écologie punitive » a été employée de manière répétée" — confirming saturation within weeks of the legislature opening. No pre-birth idiomatic background exists for this collocation. The corpus story is therefore diffusion and capture, not genesis: introduced in the AN by the centrist majority (MoDem/LREM) and LR in 2017-2018, later becoming the RN's signature anti-norm weapon from 2022 onward.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2017-10-03 — Paul-André Colombani (Non inscrits, séance publique, hydrocarbures bill): "La reconnaissance du crime d'écocide, prôné par Mme Valérie Cabanes, va dans le même sens." First sustained parliamentary episode: 2019-11-27, Christophe Bouillon's PPL "reconnaissance du crime d'écocide" (Socialist niche, commission des lois).
birth_notes: The expected birth (CCC vote of 2020-06-21) is NOT the corpus birth. "Écocide" enters the AN on 2017-10-03 via Colombani, explicitly crediting jurist Valérie Cabanes' civil-society campaign — the formula flows from NGO/legal activism into parliament, not from the CCC. The real pre-CCC champions are the Socialists: Potier (2018, 2019) and above all Christophe Bouillon, whose proposition de loi on the "crime d'écocide" was debated 2019-11-27 in commission and 2019-12-12 in séance — 7 months BEFORE the CCC voted. The CCC is an amplifier, not the origin; the 2021 loi Climat episode then becomes a metalinguistic battle over the word itself (crime vs délit): Pompili defends the "délit d'écocide" label, Untermaier says it "n'aurait aucun sens, à part de faire plaisir à la Convention citoyenne", Leseul denounces the downgrade. So the corpus genealogy is: civil society (Cabanes) → left backbenchers 2017-2019 → Socialist PPL Nov-Dec 2019 → CCC June 2020 → government co-optation/downgrade in loi Climat 2021.
notes: 45 quotes read, 43 unique (2 random samples duplicate first-15 entries: Bouillon "C'est ce constat..." and Didier Paris, both 2019-11-27). Zero false positives: "écocide" is a coined term with no competing literal sense, so the bare pattern ecocides? is safe as-is; it also catches the identical-form adjective ("projet écocide", "maire écocide", "train de vie écocide", "agenda militariste et écocide"), which is desirable — the post-2023 adjectival spread is part of the formula's diffusion. Note the pattern misses the derivative "écocidaire" if broader coverage is ever wanted, but that is an optional extension, not a fix. Usage is left-dominated (Socialistes as legislative champions, then Écologistes, LFI, Communistes, and ultramarin deputies applying it to Guyane gold-mining and Réunion projects); Renaissance/MoDem engage mainly to contest or reframe (crime→délit). Venue pattern: heavy commission concentration during the two legislative episodes (Lois 2019, commission spéciale climat 2021), then adjectival/epithet use spreads to séance publique and QAG (A69 "écocides à ciel ouvert", 2024). One meta-use worth flagging for analysis: the 2024-03-21 A69 commission witness (a mayor) reporting being called "maire écocide" — the formula quoted as an insult, evidence of activist-sphere diffusion back into parliamentary testimony.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2022-11-02 — Éric Ciotti (LR), Commission des lois, audition of Gérald Darmanin: "Et vous avez eu raison de parler d'écoterrorisme." First parliamentary occurrence is an endorsement of the minister's 3-day-old coinage, contested twice in the same session by Thomas Portes (LFI).
birth_notes: Corpus fully confirms the expected birth story and sharpens it: zero occurrences before 2022-10-30 (no pre-birth idiomatic background, not even the older US 'ecoterrorism'/ELF security-discourse sense), then the word enters the Assemblée on 2022-11-02 in Darmanin's own Commission des lois audition. Darmanin himself is not captured as a speaker in the first matches — the term is introduced into the AN record by Éric Ciotti approving it, and instantly by LFI contesting it. So in this corpus the formula is born already as a controversy, never as neutral vocabulary.
notes: Pattern quality is excellent: 37 unique quotes judged (15 first + 22 non-duplicate random samples; 8 random samples duplicate the first-15), all genuine, 0 false positives, 0 ambiguous — 'écoterrorisme' has no competing literal sense in French. Striking sociolinguistics: the term lives overwhelmingly in OPPONENTS' mouths — roughly three-quarters of matches are LFI/Écologistes/PS/PCF quoting it back critically, in scare quotes, or as accusation-recital ('vous les avez traités d'écoterroristes'), while sincere adoption is confined to RN (Jacobelli 2023-03-29 after Sainte-Soline; Magnier 2026-06-02 on farm blockades) and LR (Ciotti). Two productive ironic inversions: Panot 2022-11-09 applying it to TotalEnergies, and a 2026-02-03 speaker to wealthy alpine property investors — evidence the formula became a reusable frame, not just a label. Venue pattern: born and incubated in commissions (Lois, then the 2023 commission d'enquête on violent groupuscules where its intellectual genealogy was explicitly debated with book authors), reaching séance publique mainly from mid-2023 onward. A 2025-11-26 sentence appears twice (séance vs QAG transcript sources, 'éco-terroristes' vs 'écoterroristes') — a corpus-dedup artifact, not a pattern issue.

fp_examples: Enfin, les AESH ont été pénalisés par cette complexité nouvelle, perdant parfois des heures d’activité ou voyant leurs conditions de travail dégradées du fait de la disparition de leur temps de pause réglementaire.

pattern_fixes.detail: temps de pause (reglementaire|normative) — kills the literal labor-law sense (statutory work break, e.g. AESH break time, Portier 2024-04-03) without touching any genuine political usage; this literal sense will recur in every labor/working-conditions debate.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2023-05-17 — Sophia Chikirou (LFI), Commission des affaires européennes ("Le Président appelle à une pause réglementaire en matière environnementale"), same day as Marie Pochon (Écologistes), Aymeric Caron (LFI) and minister Olivier Becht — i.e. the AN uptake is 6 days after Macron's 2023-05-11 speech, introduced by opposition critics quoting him, not by the majority.
birth_notes: Expected birth (Macron, 2023-05-11, «pause réglementaire européenne» devant des industriels) is confirmed: zero corpus matches between 2017 and 2023-05-17, then an immediate burst. But two findings: (1) There is a genuine PRE-BIRTH antecedent — Jacques Mézard (ministre de la cohésion des territoires) already used «pause normative» on 2017-09-27 in commission développement durable, from the older norms-simplification discourse aimed at collectivités territoriales (the choc-de-simplification lineage). Macron's 2023 formula is thus a re-semantization of an existing administrative formula toward ENVIRONMENTAL deregulation, not a creation ex nihilo. Flag Mézard 2017 as background, not as the birth. (2) The formula enters the AN through its OPPONENTS: the first five AN speakers on 2023-05-17 are LFI/Écologistes quoting Macron critically or ironically (Pochon: «doit maintenant observer une "pause réglementaire" parce qu'elle menacerait notre économie»), with minister Becht defending. RN appropriates it within a week (Guiniot 2023-05-24) to claim Macron ratified their position — note RN speakers cite the speech date inconsistently («le 11 mai» Berteloot vs «le 12 mai» Guiniot).
notes: Venue pattern: virtually all matches 2023-2025 are in COMMISSIONS (affaires européennes, développement durable, affaires étrangères, finances); the phrase only reaches séance publique in 2026. Usage-mode pattern: the formula lives overwhelmingly in REPORTED SPEECH («le président de la République a appelé à...») — left uses it as an ironic indictment (Chatelain ties it to Sarkozy's «l'environnement, ça commence à bien faire»; the 2026-02-04 séance quotes still prosecute Macron with it three years later), RN uses it as vindication and fuses it with their own frame («pause réglementaire par la fin de l'écologie punitive», Loubet 2024-10-24), ministers (Becht 2023, Haddad 2025) use it as official doctrine. Diffusion: by 2023-07 it migrates beyond environment (Lottiaux applies it to housing norms), by 2024 collectivities demand a «pause normative» (Dupont/ADF), and by 2025-11-05 non-parliamentary hearing witnesses (ESS/filière actors) use it sincerely as plain lobbying language — four separate witnesses in one commission session — and it merges with the EU «simplification/omnibus» agenda (2025-04-30). The three-variant pattern (reglementaire|normative|environnementale) earns its keep: «pause normative» captures both the 2017 antecedent and the 2024-2026 spillover; no exclusions existed and only one is needed. FP rate 1/24 (~4%) — patterns are fundamentally sound, the single add_exclude is surgical.

fp_examples: Elles ont rassemblé et permis d’auditionner une grande pluralité d’acteurs, à travers la commission spécialisée du Conseil national de la transition écologique (CNTE), présidé par le sénateur Alain Richard.
L’Ademe – Agence de la transition écologique –, pour sa part, y est favorable, à l’instar du ministère de la transition écologique.

S'agissant de la remise d'un rapport au Parlement sur les réacteurs à neutrons rapides, l'article 141-2 du code de l'énergie dispose que la réalisation des objectifs fixés par les programmations pluriannuelles de l'énergie fait l'objet d'un rapport d'évaluation transmis tous les 2 ans au Conseil supérieur de l'énergie et au Conseil national de la transition écologique.

pattern_fixes.detail: Replace the pattern with: (?<!ministre de la )(?<!ministere de la )(?<!agence de la )(?<!conseil national de la )(?<!conseil national pour la )transitions? ecologiques? — Ademe's official name since 2020 ('agence de la transition ecologique') and the CNTE ('conseil national de/pour la transition ecologique') are institutional-title noise of exactly the same category as the already-excluded ministry title; using fixed-width lookbehinds (not quote-level excludes) kills only the title occurrence and preserves quotes where a genuine use co-occurs (e.g. the 2025-10-29 budget quote).

true_birth_in_corpus: 2017-07-04, Édouard Philippe (déclaration de politique générale, séance publique) — no birth event: vocabulary fully installed from the corpus's very first sitting, as expected for a baseline phrase
birth_notes: Corpus confirms the expected story exactly: 'transition écologique' is installed, consensual vocabulary from day one of the 15th legislature. First occurrences (2017-07-04) come simultaneously from the PM (3x in the politique générale speech) and the Socialist opposition (Vallaud), with GDR (Dufrègne) the next day — cross-spectrum from the start, so there is no introducer in the AN; the corpus simply begins with the term already dominant. Early government density (Lecornu 7 of the first 15) reflects the ministry portfolio and the 'contrats de transition écologique' instrument. One corpus artifact to know: Ademe was rebranded 'Agence de la transition écologique' in 2020, so post-2020 raw counts are mechanically inflated by routine agency citations until the proposed lookbehind is added.
notes: FP rate 5/45 (11%), all one clean class: official institution names (CNTE, Ademe) — same category as the ministry title the pattern already excludes by design, so the fix is a consistent extension of the existing lookbehind list. The existing ministry lookbehind verifiably works (two sampled quotes contain the ministry title but matched on a separate genuine occurrence). The 1 ambiguous is an initiative proper name ('coalition pour la transition écologique et énergétique du maritime', 2020-09-09) — too rare to warrant a rule. Usage is sincere across GDR/LFI through Renaissance/Horizons/MoDem, commission-heavy; notably ZERO RN speakers in all 45 samples — for the supposed consensual baseline, RN's apparent avoidance of the term is itself a finding worth checking against the full corpus. Mild irony appears only late (2025: 'La transition écologique européenne se fait donc grâce à un « Buy Chinese Act »'; Brocard 'dans les faits et non dans les discours'), fitting its role as the étalon that marked formulas (écologie punitive, planification écologique) later push against. Keep 'contrats de transition écologique' matches — genuine governmental deployment of the vocabulary, part of the institutionalization story.

fp_examples: Existe-t-il une solution à l’ensauvagement des marchés des matières premières ?
true_birth_in_corpus: 2017-10-18 — Marie-France Lorho (Non inscrits / Ligue du Sud, RN-sphere): "ensauvagement de notre société", in Commission des lois before Justice minister Belloubet — almost 3 years before the expected Darmanin birth (2020-07-24).
birth_notes: The corpus confirms and sharpens the expected story. (1) The formula pre-exists Darmanin in the AN: Marie-France Lorho (far-right, RN-sphere) uses it fully formed ("ensauvagement de notre société") on 2017-10-18, consistent with the word being RN/far-right lexicon since Le Pen 2013 — then total silence in the corpus for ~3 years. (2) Within the corpus, the 2020 reactivation is led by Éric Ciotti (LR) on 2020-07-20, four days BEFORE Darmanin's Figaro interview of 2020-07-24 — the LR right was already pushing the word in commission when the minister adopted it publicly. (3) Darmanin's own first corpus appearances (2020-07-28, Commission des lois, x4) are already META: he is defending his usage of the word against criticism ("ceux qui critiquent mon usage du mot"), quoting Mona Ozouf via Richard Ferrand — i.e. the controversy about the formula enters the AN simultaneously with the formula itself. (4) A distinct pre-existing register also surfaces: the geopolitical "ensauvagement du monde" (Thérèse Delpech 2005 lineage) used by CEMA Gén. Lecointre on 2020-10-15 — related linguistic background, not the domestic-security talking point (classified ambiguous).
notes: Pattern quality is excellent: the single stem pattern "ensauvag\w*" yields ~2.6% false positives on the ~39 unique quotes read (only clear FP: Tanguy/RN 2023 "ensauvagement des marchés des matières premières", an other-sense transfer to commodity markets — ironically by an RN speaker exporting his own party's lexicon). No exclusions needed; adding one for "marchés" would risk clipping legitimate compounds for a one-off. Usage sociology is striking and clean: two register camps. Sincere use = RN (Galzy, Baubry, Gillet, Taverne, Odoul, Lottiaux, Le Pen herself in 2024 "je n'ai pas peur des mots"), LR/UDR (Ciotti, Pauget, Michelet, Retailleau 2025 "France ensauvagée"), plus Ménard. Ironic/quotation/refutation use = LFI (Lachaud, Bernalicis, Martin, Panot) and PS (Houlié "je réfute fermement le terme", Benbrahim), who almost never use it except in scare quotes or to attack the government's "éléments de langage" — Bernalicis 2021 even names it explicitly as an élément de langage. One notable subversive reappropriation: Coulomme (LFI, 2024) "ensauvagement libéral" (counted genuine as dialogic reuse of the formula). Venue pattern: birth and early life in Commission des lois; broad séance-publique/QAG normalization from 2024-2026, increasingly by RN speakers where it reads as fully routinized. Data-quality flag for the corpus: at least two utterances are double-counted with different category labels (Benbrahim 2025-04-30 and Lottiaux 2026-06-30 each appear as both "Séance publique" and "Questions au gouvernement") — worth deduplicating before frequency analysis.

fp_examples: Il faut se souvenir que dans les années 1990, la Russie a été confrontée à la toute première opération de manipulation informationnelle d’ampleur sur Internet, au moment des guerres de Tchétchénie et au moment où les séparatistes tchétchènes, pour diverses raisons, ont progressivement dévié vers le djihadisme.

Il est empêtré dans des problèmes sécuritaires multiples : terrorisme, avec Boko Haram qui sévit toujours dans les Etats du Nord du pays, violents conflits entre agriculteurs et éleveurs dans la région de la Middle belt, séparatisme biafrais, piraterie maritime dans le Golfe de Guinée…
Officiellement, il s’agit de dissuader et de résister à une agression, de s’opposer à l’indépendance de Taïwan ou encore de sévir contre les mouvements séparatistes.

pattern_fixes.detail: tchetchen — observed FP (2019-12-03 'séparatistes tchétchènes', Défense commission); pure foreign-conflict sense, never co-occurs with the domestic talking point.
biafr — observed FP (2018-11-20 'séparatisme biafrais', Nigeria briefing in Affaires étrangères).
taiwan|ouighour|tibet — observed FP cluster (2019-12-11 China hearings: Taiwan independence, 'trois maux' Xinjiang framing); same foreign-geopolitics class as the existing corse/basque excludes.

donbass|donetsk|lougansk|louhansk|crimee|pro-?russes? — not in this sample but the highest-volume predictable FP source post-2022: 'séparatistes prorusses' in Ukraine-war debates; exactly the sense class already excluded for corse/catalan.
kurd|kosovo — recurrent foreign separatisms in AN foreign-affairs/defense hearings, same other-sense class; cheap insurance with no plausible overlap with the domestic formula.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2020-02-26 — Meyer Habib (UDI/Agir), commission d'enquête « Attaques de la préfecture de police » : « La radicalisation, ce que l'on appelle maintenant le séparatisme, n'est pas né en 2012 » — first genuine talking-point use, 8 days after Macron's Mulhouse speech (2020-02-18), and explicitly metalinguistic about the renaming ("ce que l'on appelle maintenant"), which confirms rather than contradicts the expected birth.
birth_notes: The expected birth (Macron, Mulhouse, 2020-02-18) holds for the islamist talking point — no genuine pre-Mulhouse use of that sense appears in the corpus, and the first AN adopter (Meyer Habib, 2020-02-26) even flags the neologism explicitly. But there is a real pre-birth parliamentary life of the word: Stéphane Peu (PCF) used « séparatisme social » / « séparatisme territorial » at least 6 times from 2018-05-17 through 2019-10-24 in the ELAN housing debates — a left-wing usage denouncing residential segregation of the poor ('parquer les plus modestes dans certains quartiers'). Macron's 2020 formula thus re-semanticized a word already circulating in the AN with a left social-critique meaning; Peu himself then re-engages the government's frame in the commission spéciale (2020-12-17: « c'est sur de telles failles... que prospère le séparatisme »). This left-wing 'séparatisme social' baseline is genuine linguistic background, not noise, and is a finding worth keeping in the genealogy.
notes: FP rate 7/45 = 15.6%, all concentrated pre-2020 and all one sense-class: foreign/territorial independence movements (Schäuble on Europe's borders 2018-01-22, Biafra, Chechnya, China/Taiwan ×2, and Letchimy's outre-mer 'séparatisme politique' ×2 on 2018-11-28). The random 2020-2026 sample is 30/30 genuine — once the law exists the word is near-unambiguous in the corpus. Two residual FPs are not keyword-fixable surgically: the Schäuble allocution (generic 'aspirations séparatistes', no country token) and Letchimy's outre-mer usage (excluding 'outre-mer' would kill genuine loi-séparatisme-in-DOM debates); accept them as residue (~4%). Usage patterns worth surfacing in the analysis: (1) venue — the formula incubates in commissions (commission spéciale « Respect des principes de la République » dominates 2020-21) before saturating séance publique; (2) it lexicalizes into legal shorthand ('la loi séparatisme', 'le délit de séparatisme', 'la loi dite séparatisme'); (3) cross-spectrum adoption — Renaissance/Gouvernement sincere use, LR pushing to broaden it, RN citing it, left using it in quotation/irony (Sebaihi 2025-01-16 'cette notion floue', the 2026-01-22 entrisme-or-séparatisme double-bind critique, the 2025-10-23 inversion 'votre séparatisme'); (4) polemical stretching by its own promoters — Darmanin 2022-11-14 calling a Green mairie's behavior 'séparatiste'; (5) a successor formula is visible at corpus end: 'séparatisme et entrisme' (Nunez 2026-04-13), suggesting 'entrisme' as the next element de langage to track.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2019-09-17 — unidentified commission witness (a researcher, colleague of François Héran, foreign-affairs commission hearing on migration): "nous, chercheurs, sommes volontiers considérés comme des bobos islamo-gauchistes" — genuine (meta) use of the political term 13 months before Blanquer. First confirmed-deputy use: Caroline Abadie (Renaissance), 2020-07-02, in scare quotes. First SINCERE deputy talking-point use: Meyer Habib (LR), 2020-11-02, to Darmanin, 11 days after Blanquer's Europe 1 statement.
birth_notes: The corpus shows a three-stage birth that inverts the expected story. (1) Pre-Blanquer (2019-09 to 2020-07), the term appears only in the mouths of its TARGETS: auditioned academics (a Héran colleague, Edgar Morin "Je ne suis pas un islamo-gauchiste") defending themselves against the label, plus Abadie (Renaissance) analyzing it in scare quotes at the racism mission — evidence the epithet already circulated publicly (Valls 2016 / Taguieff 2002 prehistory) before its ministerial consecration. (2) The Blanquer/Vidal moment registers immediately: Meyer Habib (LR) makes the first sincere deputy use on 2020-11-02 questioning Darmanin, and through 2021 the term is used overwhelmingly IRONICALLY by the left (LFI: Bernalicis, Coquerel, Panot, Mélenchon, Ressiguier; PCF: Faucillon x3) to mock Blanquer and Vidal, often in Blanquer's own hearings — the left says the word in the AN far more than the right in 2021. (3) From late 2022 the RN becomes the main sincere user (Odoul x3, Chudeau, Bordes, Sicard, Tanguy): a macronist ministerial formula migrating into an RN staple. In 2025-2026 a commission d'enquête on islamist ideology generates a meta-debate cluster about the term itself (whether it exists "de manière structurée", the morphological objection "le préfixe islamo n'a pas de sens devant le gauchisme", and the counter-coinage "islamo-droitisme").
notes: 45 quotes reviewed; 6 random samples duplicate first-15 entries, so 39 unique quotes, all genuine — the compound "islamo-gauchisme" has no literal or other-sense reading, so FP rate is 0% and the empty exclude list is correct. The pattern islamo[- ]?gauchis(me|mes|te|tes) also correctly catches the unhyphenated "islamogauchisme" variant used by Panot and Faucillon. Analysis-relevant texture: usage splits cleanly into sincere accusation (LR then RN), ironic/quotation use (LFI/PCF, including sarcasm like "madame Borne, islamo-gauchiste bien connue" 2026-01-14), and self-defense by auditioned academics; venue pattern is commissions-first (expert auditions) then séance publique. Classifying irony vs sincere use by party family would be a strong axis for the genealogy visualization.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2021-10-20 — Elsa Faucillon (GDR/PCF, commission des affaires culturelles), but in ironic quotation of Blanquer/Vidal's accusations; first SINCERE talking-point use is Julien Odoul (RN), 2022-07-12, commission des affaires culturelles.
birth_notes: Expected birth (Blanquer, 2021-07-19, outside parliament) is consistent with the corpus: zero pre-2021 background (neologism, no idiomatic baseline to rise from). The term enters the AN 3 months after Blanquer's launch — and in the very month of his colloque — via LEFT-wing critics (Faucillon PCF, twice) quoting the minister's own words back at the government ("pour reprendre vos propres mots et ceux du ministre de l'éducation nationale"). The promoters of the formula never use it first in the AN; Blanquer himself never appears in the matches. Sincere assertive use only begins 2022-07-12 with Julien Odoul (RN), i.e. once the June 2022 RN cohort arrives — the 2017-2022 legislature has no sincere use at all. So the AN genealogy is: government coins it in the media, the left imports it into parliament as an object of attack, then the RN adopts it as a sincere weapon a year later.
notes: Patterns are clean: wokisme/wokiste/woke is a French neologism with no other-sense collisions, so 0/45 false positives. The bare "woke" pattern theoretically risks English-language quotes ("I woke up") but no instance appears in 45 samples; leaving it keeps coverage of "woke culture" / "idéologie woke" / "être woke" (Chudeau 2022-07-13, Frigout 2022-11-02, Loubet quoting Ndiaye 2022-10-19). Striking usage structure: (1) strictly polarized dual use — RN + some LR use it sincerely (Odoul, Chudeau, Loubet, Lechanteux, Frigout, Jolly, Jacobelli, Parmentier, Di Filippo), while LFI/PCF/PS use it EXCLUSIVELY in scare-quotes/mockery/definition-challenge mode (Faucillon, Taché, Saintoul, Mesmeur x3, Runel, Abomangoli) — the formula never gets neutral use; (2) venue concentration in the commission des affaires culturelles et éducation (education/university/audiovisual hearings), with Chudeau and Loubet as serial users; (3) frequent explicit metadiscourse about the word itself (Saintoul asking Bolloré to define it, Mesmeur's etymology of wake, Abomangoli "ce mot en vogue chez les réactionnaires"), which is genuine talking-point material for a genealogy project; (4) data-hygiene flag: 2025-05-06 has two ASR variants of the same utterance ("au relent wokiste" vs "au Roland wokiste") — upstream dedup issue, not a pattern issue; 4 of the 30 random samples also duplicate first-15 quotes.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2023-03-28 — Pierre Meurin (RN), Commission du développement durable: « L'idéologie de la décroissance des écologistes nous entraîne vers une forme de "décivilisation" » — two months BEFORE Macron's expected birth (2023-05-24). First post-Macron echo: Alexis Corbière (LFI) on 2023-05-24 itself, the very day of the Conseil des ministres remark.
birth_notes: The expected birth (Macron, Conseil des ministres, 2023-05-24) is NOT the first corpus occurrence: RN deputy Pierre Meurin used « décivilisation » (in scare quotes, aimed at écologistes' degrowth) in commission on 2023-03-28, immediately contested by Benjamin Saint-Huile (LIOT) in the same session — evidence the Camus-adjacent term circulated in RN parliamentary discourse before the presidential adoption. This is NOT ordinary-idiom background: décivilisation was never a common French word, so the pre-birth use is already ideologically loaded. The first post-Macron parliamentary pickup is same-day (Corbière, LFI, 2023-05-24, questioning the Culture minister), showing instantaneous diffusion of the Conseil des ministres leak into the AN.
notes: Pattern decivilis\w* is essentially perfect for this neologism — zero false positives across all quotes; the \w* suffix correctly captures derived forms (décivilisées, déciviliser). Usage structure is striking: Macron dropped the formula within days, but its parliamentary life is dominated by opponents. Modes observed: (1) sincere civilizational-decline use by RN and a 2025-06-10 Lois-commission hearing witness ('racailles totalement décivilisées'); (2) hostile/ironic quotation of Macron by LFI, LR, RN (Pauget's triad décivilisation/ensauvagement/sentiment d'insécurité; Tavel's 'prétendue décivilisation'; Portes 2026 explicitly tracing it to Renaud Camus; Retailleau quoting Macron in commission); (3) a major 2025 LEFT reappropriation: the 2025-03-25 Pacte Vert debate (6 of the first 15 matches) where Dominique Potier and hearing witnesses, citing Christophe Clerc/Antoine Lyon-Caen via Norbert Elias's 'processus de civilisation', apply 'décivilisation' to the rollback of the European Green Deal — a distinct ecological second life of the formula worth surfacing in the genealogy. The single AMBIGUOUS case is Sebaihi 2026-05-28 quoting Aimé Césaire ('la colonisation travaille à déciviliser le colonisateur') — a pre-existing literary usage, though plausibly a deliberate counterpoint to the Camus/Macron lineage. Random samples overlap heavily with the first 15, indicating a small total match count (~20-25), so confidence is high.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2017-07-26 — unidentified speaker, Commission du développement durable (hearing of Catherine Guillouard, pressentie PDG RATP): "Il ne faut pas mélanger communautarisme et radicalisation…". First ATTRIBUTED genuine use: Annie Genevard (LR deputy, mislabeled "Gouvernement" in metadata), 2017-08-02, Commission affaires culturelles (audition of sports minister Laura Flessel), citing the intelligence note "Le sport amateur, vecteur de communautarisme et de radicalité" — she also produces the corpus's first "repli communautaire" the same day.
birth_notes: Consistent with the expected birth story: the formula dates to ~1989 (usage diffus) and the corpus starts in 2017, so there is no in-corpus birth to find — the word enters the corpus at its very first weeks (2017-07-26) as fully established baseline vocabulary, exactly what a "precursor" phrase should look like. What the corpus DOES capture is the substitution event the phrase was chosen for: the 2020-2021 pivot to "séparatisme". Gold evidence: Éric Poulliat (Renaissance, 2021-01-15, commission spéciale Respect des principes de la République): "une forme de séparatisme, que l'on appelait avant le communautarisme" — an explicit, on-the-record acknowledgment of the rebranding; and Marlène Schiappa (2020-11-05) naming the state's own CLIR cells "lutte contre l'islamisme et le repli communautaire", showing the second pattern captures an institutionalized administrative formula. Early corpus ownership is LR (Ciotti is the most insistent early user, 2018) plus the majority/government; RN becomes a heavy user only later in the samples (2023-2026, Odoul repeatedly).
notes: 45 quotes read (one duplicate: Diard 2019-04-03 appears in both lists). 44 genuine, 1 ambiguous, 0 false positives — the two patterns are effectively FP-free because "communautarisme/communautariste" has no literal other sense in AN speech. The single ambiguous hit is descriptive foreign-polity usage (2020-01-22, Défense commission: Lebanon's institutionalized confessionalism); at ~2% no exclusion is justified, and excluding "liban" would cut genuine France/Lebanon comparison rhetoric. Striking patterns: (1) venue — 13 of the first 15 matches are in commissions, not séance publique; the phrase lives in hearings (RATP, sport, Radio France, police) before becoming floor rhetoric; (2) the 2021 commission spéciale "Respect des principes de la République" is the hotspot where old word (communautarisme) and new word (séparatisme) coexist in the same sentences (Poulliat, Breton "entrisme communautariste et idéologies séparatistes", Attal 2025 pairing both); (3) cross-spectrum usage with distinct modes — sincere threat-framing (LR, RN, Renaissance, Gouvernement), quotation/critical mode (LFI: Coquerel quoting the exposé des motifs, Legrain contesting, though Bernalicis 2020-11-25 concedes the frame sincerely), and counter-discourse (2025-04-29 unidentified speaker attacking the "surenchère législative qui prétend combattre le communautarisme"); (4) metadata bugs to report upstream, not pattern bugs: Genevard (2017/2018) and Lescure (2018) tagged family "Gouvernement" anachronistically, and party_name typo "Les Récuplicains" for Les Républicains; also an ASR artifact "des universités et des crosses" (likely "crèches"/"campus") in Gillet 2026-05-12.

fp_examples: Je remercie Éric Poulliat, membre de votre délégation, pour ses travaux montrant la cacophonie qui peut exister entre les opérateurs, les agences et les services déconcentrés, départementaux ou régionaux, et l'espèce de sentiment de submersion qui en résulte parfois : l'élu ou l'entreprise qui a un projet se trouve confronté à un État parlant avec plusieurs voix.

pattern_fixes.detail: sentiment de submersion qui en resulte — kills the one confirmed FP (Montchalin 2020-11-17, 'sentiment de submersion' about administrative overload, no migration context) while preserving the bare 'sentiment de submersion' pattern needed for the Bayrou Jan-2025 formulation and its echoes (e.g. Pfeffer 2025-02-19).

true_birth_in_corpus: 2019-09-17 — Amélia Lakrafi (Renaissance), commission des affaires étrangères — first genuine occurrence, used in NEGATION ('il n'y a pas eu de submersion migratoire'), i.e. the formula already circulating as a media frame. First sincere affirmative use: 2021-02-03 Nicolas Dupont-Aignan (séance publique), attributing the word to Gérald Collomb. First RN use: 2022-09-20 Edwige Diaz (commission des lois).
birth_notes: Expected birth (2023-12-01, Tanguy, loi immigration) is far too late for the corpus. The phrase enters the AN in 2019 via a Renaissance MP DENYING it — evidence it was already a recognizable media/frontist frame before any parliamentary champion. NDA's 2021 sincere use explicitly sources it to Gérard Collomb (Macron's own former interior minister, 2018), not the RN. RN adoption in the AN starts with the L17→L16 cohort in Sept 2022 (Diaz), then explodes during the autumn-2023 PLF and loi-immigration debates (Gillet, T. François, Masson, Goulet + LFI/Écolo counter-uses). The Bayrou moment (LCI 27 Jan 2025 'sentiment de submersion', then QAG) is clearly visible as a second-order wave: Feb 2025 quotes from LR (Duby-Muller defending the PM's wording), LFI (Lachaud denouncing it) and RN (Pfeffer mocking 'il a osé... un sentiment de submersion'). By 2025-11-05 an opponent states on record that the expression 'est dorénavant utilisée aussi par le bloc central' — the mainstreaming is itself narrated inside the corpus. Note: pre-2025 'sentiment de submersion' without migration context is ordinary-idiom background (the Montchalin 2020 FP), not part of the political lineage.
notes: Pattern quality is excellent: 1 FP out of 44 unique quotes (~2%); one random sample duplicated a first-15 quote (Gillet 2023-10-30). All three patterns earn their keep: pattern 1 does almost all the work; pattern 3 ('submerge par l'immigration') catches genuine variants (Gillet 2025-04-08 on Mayotte); pattern 2 ('sentiment de submersion') is needed for the Bayrou formulation but is the sole FP source — hence the surgical exclude rather than dropping it. Usage patterns worth surfacing in the analysis: (1) Mayotte is the formula's anchor territory (Gillet, Bamana, and the unidentified 2025-06-11 commission speakers all tie it to Mayotte; Bayrou's own use was about Mayotte); (2) a large share of genuine matches are OPPONENTS quoting/contesting the formula (Lakrafi 2019, Lucas-Lundy, Kerbrat, Abomangoli, Lachaud, Léaument, Christophle 2026) — the phrase's parliamentary life is substantially adversarial-metalinguistic, often in scare quotes; (3) sincere users are RN + NDA, with Gillet the single most frequent voice; (4) venue drift from commission (2019-2023) to séance publique/QAG dominance after 2024, consistent with the phrase moving from niche framing to headline talking point.

fp_examples: Aujourd’hui, 90 % des embauches sont déjà faites en CDD : la création d’une sorte de CDI précaire va créer un fort appel d’air.

Assurer la prise en charge intégrale, par l’assurance maladie, des dépassements d’honoraires, des actes et des prestations liés au cancer du sein peut sembler prometteur, mais une telle mesure reviendrait à créer un appel d’air sans précédent, de nature à encourager de nombreux professionnels de santé à basculer du secteur 1 vers le secteur 2, voire à se déconventionner.
Ah non, Nasdas, tu ne dois pas aider ces jeunes-là parce que ça crée un appel d’air, un effet boule de neige, et tout le monde vient.

pattern_fixes.detail: Replace `appels? d'air` with a same-quote immigration-lexicon co-occurrence guard: `(?=[\s\S]*(migrat|migrant|asile|refugie|clandestin|sans[- ]papiers|regularis|droit du sol|passeur|frontiere|schengen|expuls|eloignement|reconduite|titres? de sejour|pays tiers|mayotte|aide medicale))[\s\S]*appels? d'air` — 'appel d'air' is a standing multi-domain French idiom (labor, fiscal, health, defense, ecology...); ~35% of raw matches are non-immigration; requiring migration vocabulary in the quote removes all 15 sampled FPs while keeping ~24/27 genuine hits. (Note: `migrat|migrant` covers immigration/migratoire/migrants without matching 'migraine'; do NOT use bare `ame` for AME — normalized 'âme' collides.)

`(mythe|fantasme|pretendus?|soi[- ]disant|formule|expression|terme)[^.]{0,40}appels? d'air|(parl|entend)\w*[^.]{0,40}(d'|de cet |de l')appels? d'air|appels? d'air[^.]{0,25}n'existe pas|n'existe pas,? l'appels? d'air|qualifier d'appels? d'air` — rescues the meta/counter-speech uses that carry no migration keyword in-quote and would be lost by the co-occurrence guard: Valls's 'la formule de l'appel d'air', Obono 'on va en entendre beaucoup parler de cet appel d'air', Lucas-Lundy 'L'appel d'air n'existe pas !' / 'Ça n'existe pas, l'appel d'air !', Martin 'Vous parlez d'appel d'air', Saint-Huile 'qualifier d'appel d'air', Faucillon 'le terme d'appel d'air'. These counter-speech quotes are analytically the most valuable and are near-exclusively about immigration in this corpus.

Apply the identical co-occurrence guard to `effets? d'appel`: `(?=[\s\S]*(migrat|migrant|asile|refugie|clandestin|sans[- ]papiers|regularis|droit du sol|passeur|frontiere|schengen|expuls|eloignement|reconduite|titres? de sejour|pays tiers|mayotte|aide medicale))[\s\S]*effets? d'appel` — 'effet d'appel' is the standard synonym in AME/asylum debates but equally generic in fiscal/economic contexts; zero of the 45 sampled quotes came from this pattern, so it is currently unverified and needs the same guard.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2018-02-07 — Fabien Di Filippo (LR), Commission des lois, debate on the European asylum regime PPL ("il existe un réel appel d'air dans des pays qui se trouvent encore en difficulté économique ; l'immigration est alors d'origine économique"). The formula arrives already contested: in the SAME session Ugo Bernalicis (LFI) rebuts it three times and Manuel Valls defends "la formule de « l'appel d'air »" as "trop floue... mais c'est une réalité".
birth_notes: The expected birth (Collomb at Calais, 2017-06-23) is extra-parliamentary and predates every corpus match. The first five corpus matches (2017-07 to 2017-11: CDD/labor, unions, tax, company resale, ecological transition) are the ordinary idiom in unrelated domains — confirming 'appel d'air' was banal multi-domain French BEFORE its immigration branding; Collomb did not coin it, he weaponized it. The first genuine parliamentary immigration use comes ~7.5 months after Collomb (Di Filippo, LR, 2018-02-07), i.e. the opposition right — not the government — imported the minister's frame into the AN, and it was contested by LFI within the hour. Crucially, unlike 'quoi qu'il en coûte', the ordinary idiom NEVER recedes: non-immigration uses persist through 2026 (bioethics 2020, payroll 2023, cancer care 2024, end-of-life 2025, NCO pay 2025, natalité 2025), so raw match counts permanently overstate the talking point by roughly a third unless the guard is applied.
notes: Counts are over the 43 unique quotes (2 random samples duplicate first-15 entries). Striking pattern: a large share of genuine immigration matches are OPPOSITIONAL quotation — LFI/PS/Écologistes/PCF negating or meta-commenting the formula ('prétendu appel d'air', 'il est faux de parler d'appel d'air', 'L'appel d'air n'existe pas !', 'expression à la mode' (Saint-Huile 2023), 'terme... très connoté politiquement' (Faucillon 2026)) — the phrase became a marked shibboleth whose contestation is itself datable. Sincere affirmative use clusters on LR/RN and interior ministers (a 2025-06-11 séance quote is a minister of the interior using it in the first person). Venue: sincere use and the corpus birth live in commission (esp. Lois); the counter-speech slogans live in séance publique. RN speakers also use the idiom generically in non-immigration domains (Ranc 2024 on healthcare), proof the generic idiom stays alive alongside the talking point. The single ambiguous quote (Bordes 2023-10-25, 'appel d'air pour les marchands de sommeil') is likely a housing bill. Caveat on the fix: the co-occurrence guard loses 2 genuine quotes of 27 (Bernalicis's scare-quoted 'profiter d'un « appel d'air » sous prétexte que la guerre fait rage' and Mendes's 'censée éviter des « appels d'air »') — an acceptable ~7% recall cost to eliminate a 35% FP rate. Alternative if the 'baseline' role is INTENTIONAL (tracking raw idiom frequency as the background the immigration formula rises from): keep patterns as-is but tag matches by debate context downstream; as shipped, however, the raw stream is unusable as a pure immigration-cadrage signal.

true_birth_in_corpus: 2017-07-11, unidentified speaker (Commission des lois, audition de Gérard Collomb) — first corpus match, and already in the 'execution is weak' frame ("les OQTF sont extrêmement faibles"). First named, fully-formed inexécution talking point: Éric Ciotti (LR), 2018-04-03, Commission des lois ("sur 92 000 obligations de quitter le territoire français prononcées, à peine 17 000 sont menées à leur terme").
birth_notes: The expected birth (2022-10-18, post-meurtre-de-Lola) is CONFIRMED in the samples — Timothée Houssin (RN) uses the fait-divers framing that exact day ("une clandestine algérienne... qui faisait l'objet d'une OQTF"). But the corpus reveals a long parliamentary prehistory: continuous use since 2017-07-11, dense in the 2018 loi asile-immigration debates (Collomb bill, Commission des lois). Crucially, the 'inexécution des OQTF' statistical argument — usually attributed to the post-Lola moment — is fully formed in Éric Ciotti's (LR) mouth on 2018-04-03 (92 000 prononcées / 17 000 exécutées; 96 % des déboutés se maintiennent) and even in the very first 2017 match. So 2022 is not the birth of the term (legal creation 2006) nor of the inexécution argument (LR, 2018); it is the birth of a MUTATION: OQTF shifts from policy-metric to identity-tag for perpetrators/victims of faits divers ('sous OQTF' — Houssin 2022, Gillet 2023 'des Français victimes d'étrangers objets d'une OQTF', Diaz 2024, Barnier 2026 'Il était sous une OQTF.'). Also notable: a left-critical usage predating the RN frame (2021-09-08, commission d'enquête migrants: 'les OQTF tombent tous les jours pour des jeunes scolarisés et diplômés'). Since OQTF has no other sense, all pre-2022 technical/legislative uses count as genuine linguistic background, not false positives.
notes: Patterns are surgical: 'oqtf' is a unique acronym; 'obligations? de quitter le territoire' is an unambiguous legal phrase — zero false positives across all 45 quotes, no exclusions needed. Analysis-worthy signals: (1) venue migration — pre-2022 usage is almost entirely Commission (esp. Lois); Séance publique and QAG appearances concentrate 2024+ (Diaz, Berrios, Rodwell, Mazaury), i.e. the term moves from committee-room jargon to hemicycle rhetoric; (2) full cross-spectrum adoption post-2022, including contestation: LFI (Portes 2023 'allez au bout de votre logique', Léaument 2025 quoting and rebutting the RN's 'tué par quelqu'un sous OQTF' trope), Écologistes (Sebaihi 2025 'OQTF cyniques' — inverted critical use), PS (Christophle 2024 attacking the 'politique du chiffre' behind execution rates) — the left now debates ABOUT the formula rather than avoiding it; (3) government speakers (Collomb 2018, Darmanin 2023) use it technically/defensively while LR/RN drive the offensive framing; (4) meta-discourse appears by 2025 ('on en parle beaucoup des OQTF', Le Bras hearing) — a sign of full lexicalization. For the genealogy: treat 2006 as legal creation, 2017-2018 as parliamentary politicization of inexécution (LR-led), 2022-10 as the fait-divers mutation (RN-led, then mainstream).

true_birth_in_corpus: 2018-02-14, Isabelle Rauch (Renaissance/Horizons), Commission des affaires étrangères, OFPRA hearing — first immigration-framed use ("ponts entre demande d'asile et immigration économique... demandeurs susceptibles de répondre aux métiers en tension"). First raw match is one day earlier (2018-02-13, Fabrice Le Vigoureux) but in the ordinary labor sense (public-sector data analysts).
birth_notes: The expected birth (Darmanin/Dussopt, Le Monde, 2022-11-02) is NOT the phrase's birth — it is the moment an existing administrative term became the name of a flagship legislative measure (titre de séjour "métiers en tension"). The corpus shows: (1) continuous ordinary labor-economics background since Feb 2018 (Pénicaud on formation/CPF/apprentissage, defense recruitment, Pôle emploi #VersUnMétier) — this is genuine linguistic baseline, not noise; (2) the immigration framing is ALREADY present in Feb-Oct 2018: Rauch (2018-02-14, asile/immigration économique), then the loi Collomb debates (2018-03-27/04-06: Perrut, Stella Dupont, Mörch) discuss regularization EXPERIMENTS tied to métiers en tension and low-unemployment territories — a direct parliamentary ancestor of article 3/4 bis of the 2023 law, four and a half years early; (3) Clapot (2018-10-16) cites the 2008 official list of 30 métiers en tension, confirming the term predates the corpus as administrative vocabulary. The Nov 2022 "birth" is thus a rebranding/escalation: the first post-interview talking-point echo in the samples is Desjonquères (MoDem, 2022-11-23, "création d'un titre de séjour pour les métiers en tension").
notes: No strict false positives: the phrase has a single sense (occupations with recruitment shortages), so every match is semantically correct. The 9 AMBIGUOUS quotes are post-birth uses in the ordinary labor/training/defense sense with no immigration link (e.g. Dussopt 2023-09-18 on formation, Gruet 2024-05-07 on training Jura youth, Dessigny 2024-11-05 on apprentissage funding, Girard 2024-11-27 and Vautrin 2026-05-04 on defense-industry recruitment, Taché 2025-06-25 on teacher shortages). This ~20% dilution is NOT regex-fixable: genuine talking-point uses frequently carry no immigration keyword in-sentence (Darmanin 2023-11-30: "le texte concerne les métiers en tension, lesquels ne sont pas si nombreux"; Mandon 2023-11-21: "le compromis trouvé au Sénat... ouvre un chemin"), so any add_exclude on formation/apprentissage/défense or a required immigration-context window would destroy real coverage. Recommendation for the analysis layer: treat this phrase as a politicization-of-a-technical-term case — the story is the frequency spike + venue shift (commission-heavy technical use 2018-2022, then séance publique + loi immigration debate density from Nov 2022) + speaker-mix change, not mere presence.
